channel.title: Daily AI Digest
channel.description: Curated AI/ML signals — zero LLMs, pure algorithmic curation

item.title: I am building a cloud
item.description: 💬 Major HN discussion (118 comments)

item.title: Reward Hacking in the Era of Large Models: Mechanisms, Emergent Misalignment, Challenges
item.description: Reward Hacking in the Era of Large Models: Mechanisms, Emergent Misalignment, Challenges

item.title: Cortex 2.0: Grounding World Models in Real-World Industrial Deployment
item.description: 📄 Featured on Hugging Face Daily Papers — Industrial robotic manipulation demands reliable long-horizon execution across embodiments, tasks, and changing object distributions. While Vision-Language-Action models have demonstrated strong generalization, they remain fundamentally reactive. By optimizing the next action given the current ob...

item.title: AI scientists produce results without reasoning scientifically
item.description: AI scientists produce results without reasoning scientifically

item.title: LLaDA2.0-Uni: Unifying Multimodal Understanding and Generation with Diffusion Large Language Model
item.description: 📄 New in cs.CV — We present LLaDA2.0-Uni, a unified discrete diffusion large language model (dLLM) that supports multimodal understanding and generation within a natively integrated framework. Its architecture combines a fully semantic discrete tokenizer, a MoE-based dLLM backbone, and a diffusion decoder. By dis...

item.title: Convergent Evolution: How Different Language Models Learn Similar Number Representations
item.description: 📄 New in cs.CL, cs.AI — Language models trained on natural text learn to represent numbers using periodic features with dominant periods at $T=2, 5, 10$. In this paper, we identify a two-tiered hierarchy of these features: while Transformers, Linear RNNs, LSTMs, and classical word embeddings trained in different ways al...

item.title: SWE-chat: Coding Agent Interactions From Real Users in the Wild
item.description: 📄 New in cs.AI, cs.CY — AI coding agents are being adopted at scale, yet we lack empirical evidence on how people actually use them and how much of their output is useful in practice. We present SWE-chat, the first large-scale dataset of real coding agent sessions collected from open-source developers in the wild. The d...

item.title: Near-Future Policy Optimization
item.description: 📄 New in cs.LG — Reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards (RLVR) has become a core post-training recipe. Introducing suitable off-policy trajectories into on-policy exploration accelerates RLVR convergence and raises the performance ceiling, yet finding a source of such trajectories remains the key challeng...

item.title: SpeechParaling-Bench: A Comprehensive Benchmark for Paralinguistic-Aware Speech Generation
item.description: 📄 New in cs.CL, cs.AI — Paralinguistic cues are essential for natural human-computer interaction, yet their evaluation in Large Audio-Language Models (LALMs) remains limited by coarse feature coverage and the inherent subjectivity of assessment. To address these challenges, we introduce SpeechParaling-Bench, a comprehen...

item.title: DeVI: Physics-based Dexterous Human-Object Interaction via Synthetic Video Imitation
item.description: 📄 New in cs.CV — Recent advances in video generative models enable the synthesis of realistic human-object interaction videos across a wide range of scenarios and object categories, including complex dexterous manipulations that are difficult to capture with motion capture systems. While the rich interaction know...

item.title: Parallel-SFT: Improving Zero-Shot Cross-Programming-Language Transfer for Code RL
item.description: 📄 New in cs.CL — Modern language models demonstrate impressive coding capabilities in common programming languages (PLs), such as C++ and Python, but their performance in lower-resource PLs is often limited by training data availability. In principle, however, most programming skills are universal across PLs, so ...

item.title: microsoft / ai-agents-for-beginners
item.description: ⭐ 1.1k GitHub stars today · ⭐ 58.4k total stars — 12 Lessons to Get Started Building AI Agents

item.title: mvanhorn / last30days-skill
item.description: ⭐ 257 GitHub stars today · ⭐ 23.6k total stars — AI agent skill that researches any topic across Reddit, X, YouTube, HN, Polymarket, and the web - then synthesizes a grounded summary

item.title: Why Single-Agent AI is Dead: Inside Anthropic’s New Blueprint for Long-Running Agents
item.description: 🆕 New article — <div class="medium-feed-item"><p class="medium-feed-image"><a href="https://medium.com/@richardhightower/why-single-agent-ai-is-dead-inside-anthropics-new-blueprint-for-long-running-agents-2f897d34d247?source=rss------ai_agent-5"><img src="https://cdn-images-1.medium.com/max/1376/1*rcrdVm-fAs8Ns2...

item.title: evidentlyai / evidently
item.description: 💻 Trending on GitHub — Evidently is ​​an open-source ML and LLM observability framework. Evaluate, test, and monitor any AI-powered system or data pipeline. From tabular data to Gen AI. 100+ metrics.

item.title: kyegomez / swarms
item.description: ⭐ 65 stars today on GitHub — The Enterprise-Grade Production-Ready Multi-Agent Orchestration Framework. Website:https://swarms.ai
